login.title: Teamwork
login.subtitle: Enter your secret key to continue
login.noKeyYet: No key yet? Create or join a team
login.invalidKey: Invalid key or server not running

dashboard.welcomeBody: This is your team's shared memory for Claude conversations. Say "sync" or "sync to Router" — or Claude will ask when it senses a good moment.
dashboard.noEntriesBody: Once your team starts syncing, conversations will appear here.
dashboard.deletePresetBody: This pinned tag combination will be removed from your shortcuts.
dashboard.mcpUpdateBodyPre: Reconnect MCP once — restart Claude Code / Desktop, or disconnect + reconnect in claude.ai → Connectors. Or try the new
dashboard.tagMigrationBody: All of your channel configs are preserved — webhooks, Lark bindings, and skills moved over as-is. Click a #tag chip on any entry, or the new "Tags" nav link, to manage them.

entry.hiddenBadge: Hidden — only you can see this
entry.commentPlaceholder: Write a comment… Type @ to mention (Cmd+Enter to send)
entry.editChannelHint: Leave empty to remove from tag
entry.deleteConfirmBody: This entry will be permanently deleted. This action cannot be undone.
entry.deleteCommentBody: This comment will be permanently deleted.
entry.adminEditConfirm: You are editing @{handle}'s entry as an admin. Continue?
entry.adminDeleteConfirmBody: You are about to delete another member's entry as an admin. This cannot be undone.

register.joinTeamSubtitle: Ask your team admin for an invite code
register.chooseBody: Create a new team, or join an existing one with an invite code.
register.saveKeyBody: This key is your identity. If you lose it, you cannot recover your account. Save it to a password manager now.
register.connectClaudeBody: Add Router as an MCP server in your Claude client.
register.autoSkillNote: Router's default skill is auto-loaded from the MCP server — no CLAUDE.md copy needed. Personalize it later in Settings.
register.trySayTitle: Say "sync" or "sync to Router" — or let Claude ask
register.tryBody: Claude will generate a summary, auto-tag it, and post to your team notebook.
register.alreadyHaveKey: Already have a key? Go to Dashboard
register.failedServer: Failed to connect to server. Is the backend running?
register.saveKeyWarning: Save your secret key — you won't see it again
register.keepSafe: This key is your identity. Keep it safe, don't share it.
register.connectSubtitle: Choose your client to get setup instructions
register.step1Run: Run this command in your terminal:
register.goToConnectorsHint: Go to Settings → Connectors → Add custom connector
register.autoSkillShort: Connected — Router's default skill is auto-loaded from the MCP server. To personalize later, open Settings and find "teach Router your style".
register.tryItOutBody: You're all set. Here's how to use Router:
register.step1Desc: Discuss anything — code, design, product ideas
register.step2Title: Say "sync" / "sync to Router" — or let Claude ask
register.step2Desc: Claude generates a summary, auto-tags it, and posts to your team notebook
register.step3Desc: Filter by tags, search by keyword, see what your team is working on
register.exampleSummary: Summary: H5 homepage uses Swiper for full-screen scroll…

settings.title: Settings
settings.profileHint: Public info visible to your teammates.
settings.publishingDelayHint: How long entries stay pending before publishing. You can delete or publish immediately during this window.
settings.personalWebhookHint: When someone @-mentions you or comments on your entry, Router will POST a notification to this URL. Works with Lark, Slack, Telegram bots, or any JSON webhook.
settings.personalWebhookLarkStep1: Create a new Lark group with just yourself in it
settings.personalWebhookLarkStep2: Group settings → Bots → Add bot → Custom Bot
settings.personalWebhookLarkStep3: Copy the generated webhook URL and paste it below
settings.personalWebhookLarkStep4: Mentions will land in that single-person group — like a personal DM
settings.larkNotifNotBound: Lark account not linked — Lark IM notifications are unavailable.
settings.larkNotif_comment: Push when someone comments on my entry
settings.larkNotif_reply: Push when someone replies to my comment
settings.larkNotif_digest: Push the Router Weekly Brief on Monday 10am
settings.connectClaudeHint: Set up your Claude client to sync with the team notebook.
settings.connectedOut: That's it — sync instructions are auto-loaded from the MCP server when Claude connects.
settings.personalizeTitle: Optional: teach Router your style (personal template)
settings.personalizeBody: Paste this template into ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md and fill in the 👉 marks. Delete any section you don't want.
settings.guideSubtitle: From your perspective — when things get recorded, how to sync manually, where to find things.
settings.scenario1Body: When you tell Claude to sync, it writes the entry right away — no confirmation needed:
settings.scenario2Body: When the conversation hits a natural wrap-up signal, Claude will lightly ask "Sync this to Router?" at the end of its reply:
settings.scenario2Signal1: Decision landed — "let's go with this", "final call"
settings.scenario2Signal3: Problem solved — bug fixed, feature working, deploy succeeded
settings.scenario2Hint: Say "yes" / "sync" to confirm, or "no" / "skip" to drop it. Claude only asks once per conversation.
settings.scenario3Title: Scenario 3 — What happens after a sync
settings.scenario3Step1: Claude's reply includes: ✅ synced, the summary, the tags + reason, entry ID
settings.scenario3Step2: The entry enters a 15-minute staging window where only you can see it
settings.scenario3Step3: During staging you can delete it, publish immediately, or let it auto-publish
settings.scenario3Step4: After 15 minutes it's visible to the whole team and may trigger tag webhooks / notifications
settings.scenario4Title: Scenario 4 — What Router will NOT sync
settings.scenario4Body1: Pure execution — editing files, running commands, fixing typos
settings.scenario4Body2: Casual chat with no decision or insight
settings.scenario4Body3: Anything you explicitly say "don't record" / "skip this"
settings.browseDashboard: Dashboard — filter by tags, search by keyword, pin presets
settings.browseMembers: Members — see what teammates are working on
settings.browseStaging: You can delete or edit any pending entry in the 15-min window.
settings.codexAlsoFor: Codex CLI uses TOML for MCP servers. The CLI tab is simpler if you can use it.
settings.codexNote: Note: if Codex doesn't auto-recognize sync phrases, tell it explicitly: "when I say sync, call router_write".
settings.cursorAlsoFor: Same JSON shape works for Windsurf and most other MCP-via-JSON clients — paste into their Settings → MCP.
settings.cursorNote: Note: if Cursor doesn't auto-recognize sync phrases, tell it explicitly: "when I say sync, call router_write".
settings.personalizeHint: Below is an annotated template. Paste into ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, then fill in the 👉 marks — trigger words, common tags, summary style, things you don't want synced. Any section left empty or removed falls back to the default.
settings.defaultSkillPrompt: Want to see what the default skill looks like?
settings.inviteMembersHint: Generate an invite link for your teammates. They open it and the code is pre-filled — no copying by hand.
settings.rawInviteCode: Raw invite code (if someone can't open the link)

sync.title: Sync settings
sync.subtitle: Control how Claude Code / Codex push conversations to Router. Changes take effect within 5 minutes across all terminals — no skill reinstall needed.
sync.modeActiveDesc: AI proactively asks when it sees a noteworthy moment, or pushes immediately when you say "sync".
sync.modePassiveDesc: AI auto-pushes silently with privacy stripping. You get a notification afterward; undo within 15 minutes.
sync.previewAlways: Always show a preview and wait for my confirmation
sync.previewNever: Push directly, then tell me what was pushed
sync.saved: ✓ Saved (CC/Codex picks up the new setting within 5 min)

settings.cliSectionHint: One-command setup for Claude Code and Codex CLI. Skill template auto-syncs in the background. Doesn't work in Claude Desktop or claude.ai web (no shell).
settings.connectClaudeLegacyHint: Works in Claude Code, Claude Desktop, claude.ai web, Codex, and Cursor.
settings.secretKeyEmpty: No key visible in this browser
settings.secretKeyEmptyHint: Your account has a key on the server (stored as a hash, never plaintext). To make it visible here:
settings.secretKeyEmptyOpt1Pre: Paste an existing key (from another browser or ~/.routerrc) above in
settings.secretKeyEmptyOpt2: Click "Rotate MCP credential" above to generate a new one (invalidates any existing CLI / MCP setup using the old key)
settings.secretKeyEmptyOpt3Post: in the terminal — handles login + key in one go
settings.cliMcpMigrationTitle: Already had Router MCP added in Claude Code itself?
settings.cliMcpMigrationBody: If you only need MCP for Desktop / Web (via claude.ai Connectors), you can drop the duplicate from Claude Code:
settings.cliMcpMigrationFootnote: This only removes the project-level MCP in Claude Code — your claude.ai Connector (Desktop / Web) stays untouched.
settings.cliClientsTip: Claude Desktop / claude.ai web don't support CLI — use MCP for those.

channels.title: Tag management
channels.timelineEmpty: No node entries in the last {days} days.
channels.timelineHint: Tag an entry with decision / milestone / shipped / release / incident / retro to surface it here.
channels.deleteChannelBody: The tag will be deleted, but all entries inside it are kept — they still show on the Dashboard and keep their tags. This cannot be undone.
channels.noChannels: No tags yet. Create one to get started.
channels.noEntriesInChannel: No entries in this tag yet.
channels.noSkillsHint: Add a Tag Skill (Claude applies it before posting) or a Webhook Skill (server pushes to Lark / HTTP).

skillForm.kindRewriteDesc: A free-form briefing for Claude — background, terminology, rules, lookups. Claude must read it before posting to this tag.
skillForm.kindWebhookDesc: When an entry lands, the server pushes it to a Lark bot / HTTP endpoint. Filter by tag or author.
skillForm.name: Name
skillForm.instructionsPlaceholder:
Write anything you want Claude to know or do before posting to this tag. Mix background, rules, lookups — whatever makes sense. Example:

## Background
My Project is an information-stream product, React + Next.js 16. Core modules: infinite-scroll, story-card, tag-bar.
Glossary: 'infinite-scroll' not 'infinite scroll'; 'story card' not 'card'.

## Before writing
- Search this tag for related entries from the past week; if you find one, cite it in the summary ("continues @alex's xxx").
- If the entry directly follows an existing one, link its ID in the content.

## Format & tags
- Technical decisions: Problem / Options / Pick / Why.
- Anything about perf or loading must include #perf.
- Anything about a11y must include #a11y.
- No emoji.

## Default role
Default to the frontend perspective unless the content is clearly backend.
skillForm.instructionsHint: Anyone in the team can edit this, and changes take effect immediately (no MCP reconnect needed). Claude will read the skill and pick whichever tools are right on its own — describe intent in plain language, not function names.
skillForm.webhookUrlHint: Lark group bot or any HTTP webhook.
skillForm.triggerHint: Fires when ANY filter matches (OR): the entry includes any of the tags OR any of the authors.
skillForm.filterTagsPlaceholder: leave empty to not filter by tag
skillForm.filterAuthorsPlaceholder: leave empty to not filter by author
skillForm.kindDigestDesc: Auto-generates a weekly or monthly summary of the tag's entries using AI.
skillForm.digestPostToChannel: Post digest as an entry in this tag
skillForm.digestWebhookHint: Also send the digest to a Lark group or HTTP endpoint.
skillForm.digestInstructionsPlaceholder:
Default template (used when left empty):

## {Weekly/Monthly} Digest — #{tag}

### By Author
Group by author. For each: name, entry count, 1-2 sentence highlights.

### Key Decisions
Entries about decisions, architecture choices, direction changes.

### Open Items
Anything tagged #blocker, #urgent, or #review-needed.

### Summary
2-3 sentence overview of the tag's focus.

---
To customize, replace the above with your own instructions.
skillForm.digestInstructionsHint: Leave empty to use the default template shown above. Write your own to override — e.g. group by module, focus on blockers, write in a specific language.

members.title: Members
members.removeConfirmBody: Their entries will remain in the team feed, but they will lose access. This cannot be undone.
members.lastAdminHint: At least one admin must remain on the team.
members.promoteConfirmBody: They will be able to edit/delete any entry, remove members, and manage admin status team-wide.
members.demoteConfirmBody: They will lose admin privileges. You can promote them again later.

memory.sectionDesc: Admin-maintained markdown that gives Claude Code team-wide context (people, tech stack, conventions, long-term goals). Sections are loaded on demand by CC.
memory.pageDesc: Static markdown loaded into Claude Code's system context at session start. Keep it concise (under {limit} chars).
memory.notConfigured: ⚠ Memory not configured yet — Claude Code has no team context for your team. Click "Show example" below for a starter template you can paste in and adapt.
memory.placeholder:
# Team Memory

## People
- @hx — full-stack lead
- ...

## Tech Stack
- Frontend: Next.js + Zustand
- ...

(See "Show example" below for the full starter template.)
memory.exampleNote: Reference only — copy what's useful, adapt the rest. Memory is loaded by CC verbatim, so keep it real.
memory.readonlyDesc: — only team admins can edit Memory.
memory.noAdmins: No admin found in your team — please reach out to whoever set up Router for you.
memory.restoreConfirmMessage: The current content will be saved as the new previous version, so you can re-toggle if needed.

concierge.sectionDesc: Per-user weekly recap pushed Monday 10am Beijing — team activity overview + a personal callout connecting your work to what others shipped. Mentions and replies stay on the realtime push tag.
concierge.pageDesc: The weekly brief is a per-user push that arrives every Monday 10am Beijing time. It uses an LLM to synthesize a short team-activity overview plus a 1-2 sentence callout connecting your recent work to what teammates have shipped.
concierge.toggleHint: When on, you receive the weekly brief in your inbox and via Lark IM (if Lark is bound). When off, neither inbox nor Lark push happens. The on-demand router_brief MCP tool still works regardless.
concierge.howIntro: Computed once per week from the last 7 days of activity. Recap groups (capped at 5 items each):
concierge.groupMentioned: @ you — entries that mention your handle (counted in footer; not pushed again)
concierge.groupReplied: Replies to you — comments on entries you wrote (counted in footer; not pushed again)
concierge.groupChannels: Subscribed tags — new entries in tags you joined
concierge.groupMilestones: Team milestones — entries tagged decision / shipped / incident / milestone / release
concierge.groupTopics: Related to your topics — entries on tags you used recently (by others)
concierge.howOutro: Skipped weeks: if subscribed tags + milestones + related topics are all empty for the week, no card is sent. Mentions/replies are excluded from the skip count because they're already pushed in realtime.

bookmarks.title: Bookmarks
bookmarks.emptyHint: Click the bookmark icon on any entry to save it here.
bookmarks.emptyHintShort: Click the bookmark icon on any entry to save it here

feedback.title: Send feedback
feedback.placeholder: What's on your mind? Bugs, ideas, anything. Cmd+Enter to submit.

search.hint: Type keywords to search entries, or @handle to find a user.
search.hintPrefix: Type keywords to search entries, or

tags.deleteTagConfirmBody: "#{name}" will be removed from all entries. This cannot be undone.
tags.mergeConfirmBody: All entries tagged "#{from}" will be retagged to "#{to}". The old tag disappears.
tags.deleteSingleBody: This tag will be removed from every entry that uses it. This cannot be undone.
tags.mergeRelabelBody: {sources} → all matching entries will be relabeled as #{to}.
tags.renameEmpty: New tag name cannot be empty.
tags.renameInvalid: Use lowercase letters, digits, hyphens and colons only.
tags.renamePlainBody: All entries tagged #{from} will be relabeled #{to}. No existing #{to} tag exists yet.
tags.renameMergeBody: Tag #{to} already exists — renaming will merge #{from} into #{to}. All entries tagged #{from} will be relabeled #{to}.
tags.isPresetTag: This is a preset tag — it already exists.

lark.loginUnavailable: Lark not configured — contact your admin
lark.errorNotBound: This Lark account isn't linked to any router account. Sign in with your secret_key first, then bind from /settings.
lark.errorInvalidState: Sign-in link expired, please try again
lark.errorAlreadyBoundOnInvite: This Lark account is already linked to router user @{handle}. To join a new team via this invite, use a different Lark account. To log in as @{handle}, click "Sign in with Lark" on the home page (don't go through the invite link).

register.title: Complete Lark registration
register.expiredBody: Lark sign-in took longer than 10 minutes to complete. Please try again.
register.handleLabel: Pick a router handle (3-15 chars, lowercase letters + digits)
register.inviteHint: Lark registration must use an invite code to join an existing team. To create a new team, use the {link} flow.
register.errorHandleFormat: Handle must be 3-15 chars, lowercase alphanumeric + underscore, starting with a letter.
register.errorInviteRequired: Please enter the team invite code.
register.errorHandleTaken: Handle @{handle} is already taken — pick another.
register.errorBoundElsewhere: This Lark account is already bound to a different router account.
register.doneSubtitle: Lark account bound and joined team {teamId}. Browser sign-in uses cookies — you'll be auto-logged-in next time.
register.doneStep1Hint: If you don't save it now, you can generate a new one anytime from {settings}. The browser doesn't need it for sign-in.
register.doneStep2Title: Step 2: connect Claude Code (optional, only if you want CC to call router)
register.doneStep2OtherTitle: Use Codex / Cursor / another MCP client?

settings.unbindConfirm: After unbinding, Lark sign-in will be unavailable until you re-bind. Continue?
settings.benefit1: Sign in to router with your Lark account
settings.benefit3: Get @mentioned in Lark when assigned router tasks
settings.bindConflict: This Lark account is already bound to a different router account

mcp.intro: Browser sign-in uses cookie sessions and doesn't touch your secret_key. The secret_key is only used by CC / Codex / scripts to talk to MCP.
mcp.whenToClickItem1: Direct-Lark-register users connecting to CC MCP for the first time — you've never been shown a key before
mcp.whenToClickItem2: Suspect your key was leaked and want to invalidate it immediately
mcp.whenToClickItem3: Setting up CC on a new machine and don't want to share the key with the old one
mcp.whenNotToClickItem1: CC is already running and you want to keep it working — old credential gets a 7-day grace then expires, your old CC will disconnect
mcp.saveKeyAlert: Save this key now — it won't be shown again:
mcp.graceWarn: Old credential works for 7 days during grace, then expires. Update all MCP clients.
mcp.rotateConfirm: After generating a new credential, the old one will expire after a 7-day grace period. Continue?
mcp.pasteHint: (stored in a password manager / CC config / notes)
mcp.pasteBody: Paste it back → we verify it really belongs to you → this device can render commands again. **No rotation, doesn't affect other devices**.
mcp.verifyOk: ✓ Restored. Commands below now include your key.
mcp.verifyWrong: ✗ This key doesn't belong to the current account.
mcp.noKeyNotice: **No secret_key** — you're a Lark-only user. The placeholders below aren't usable. Go up to {link} above to generate one, then come back to copy.

bindings.title: Lark group bindings
bindings.subtitle: Two-way bridge between this tag and a Lark group chat — push entries to the group, summarize discussions back into the tag.
bindings.helpStep1Title: 1. Add Router Bot to your Lark group
bindings.helpStep1Body: Search for "Router Bot" in your Lark org and invite it into the group you want to bridge.
bindings.helpStep2Title: 2. Connect the group to this tag
bindings.helpStep2Body: In the group, send: @bot /connect {channelId} — the binding will appear here.
bindings.helpStep3Body: Use the toggles below per binding to decide whether router entries auto-push to the group, whether the bot watches the group for signals, and where /summarize archives entries.
bindings.emptyTitle: No groups bound to this tag yet
bindings.emptyHint: Once a Lark group connects with @bot /connect {channelId}, it will show up here.

card.unbindConfirm: Disconnect this Lark group? It will stop receiving entries and the binding will be deleted.
card.pushBody: When new entries are written in this tag, send them as cards to the group. Default: off.
card.watchBody: Bot quietly observes the group and only speaks up when there's something worth flagging — undocumented decisions, unanswered questions, stuck handoffs. Default: off.
card.archiveBody: When @bot /summarize runs in the group, the entry is saved to this tag.
card.styleBody: Controls how /summarize organizes the Updates section.
card.watchObsEmpty: No observations yet — bot has been silent.

setup.title: Setup
setup.subtitle: Connect Router to your AI tools. Pick CLI if your client supports it, MCP otherwise.
setup.cliBodyPost: — it opens your browser to log in, then installs the Skill into Claude Code (or Codex, CLI or desktop).
setup.cliFootnotePre: After init, your AI will proactively ask to sync noteworthy moments — or just say
setup.cliClientNoteTitle: CLI works only with Claude Code and Codex (CLI or desktop)
setup.cliClientNoteBody: If you use Claude Desktop or claude.ai web, switch to the MCP option — that's the only path that works on those clients.
setup.mcpBody: Works in every MCP client — Claude Code, Claude Desktop, claude.ai web, Codex, Cursor.
setup.othersManualPath: Other MCP-via-SSE clients (Windsurf, Continue, Zed, etc.):
setup.othersHint: Paste this URL into your client's MCP server config — consult its docs for exact format (JSON, TOML, YAML, etc.).
setup.desktopMemoryNoteTitle: Heads up: Team Memory needs manual load on Desktop
setup.desktopMemoryNoteBody: Claude Desktop / claude.ai web don't auto-inject the team Memory the way Claude Code (CLI) does. After connecting, type / in your conversation and pick the team_memory slash command to load Memory into context — or just ask the assistant to load the team Memory. This is a Desktop client limitation, not a router setup issue. CLI users get Memory automatically, no extra steps.
setup.legacySseBody: If your MCP client doesn't yet support the http transport above, fall back to the legacy SSE URL. SSE works the same way functionally but is being phased out — switch to http when your client supports it.
setup.settingsLinkPre: Full docs and key rotation are in
setup.notLoggedInLabel: Paste your key on the home page first

cliCallback.title: Router CLI Login
cliCallback.sending: Sending key back to your terminal...
cliCallback.doneFallback: If it doesn't close automatically, you can close it manually.
cliCallback.rotateNoticeTitle: About to generate a new key
cliCallback.rotateNoticeBody: This will replace your existing key. Any current MCP / CLI session using the old key will need to be reconfigured with the new one. (Multi-key per user is on the v1.1 roadmap.)

setup.cliClientsTip: Doesn't work in Claude Desktop or claude.ai web — those need MCP.
setup.mcpClientsTip: Works in Claude Desktop, claude.ai web, and Claude Code. The tool definitions load each turn, so token usage is higher than CLI.
